# tela — the team wiki your agents can write to

> A self-hostable, markdown-native team wiki with a built-in MCP server, so AI agents read, write, and search your docs as first-class teammates. Live multiplayer, ranked full-text + semantic search, your data on your server.

## What tela is

tela is an open-source, self-hostable **agent-native** team wiki. The wedge: a built-in Model Context Protocol (MCP) server makes AI agents first-class participants — they read, write, and search the wiki through the same API the team's UI uses, not through a bolted-on chat box.

- **Markdown is canonical, forever.** `pages.body` is plain markdown text — no block model, no proprietary format. The Milkdown editor is fully WYSIWYG; the file underneath stays clean markdown. Bulk-import a directory, export any time. Leaving tela means copying files.
- **Self-hosted, your data.** Go backend on PostgreSQL; full-text search is native Postgres (ranked `ts_rank_cd` over a generated `tsvector`), with optional semantic/RAG retrieval via an embedding model — no Elasticsearch, no external search service. `docker compose up`, one host port, your data on your disk.
- **Real-time multiplayer.** Live collaborative editing over Yjs — concurrent cursors and edits, rebased onto the canonical markdown on save. Comments anchor to a `{prefix, exact, suffix}` text window, so they don't drift when the page is edited.
- **Built for agents.** Ships `tela-mcp` (published on npm). Agents authenticate with a scoped personal access token and get 20 tools: search (ranked full-text), semantic_search + read_chunk (vector + keyword retrieval with cited chunks), get_page, list_pages, list_backlinks, fetch, create_page, update_page (auto-snapshots a revision), move_page, add_comment (text-anchored), import_mira (bulk), submit_feedback, plus space management. Keys are scoped read / write / admin and can be pinned to a single space.

## Stack
- Backend: Go + PostgreSQL (`pgx/v5`). Search: ranked Postgres full-text (`tsvector` / `ts_rank_cd`) plus optional semantic/RAG retrieval.
- Frontend: React + TypeScript + Vite + Tailwind + the Milkdown editor; Yjs for live collaboration.
- MCP server: TypeScript, published as `tela-mcp` on npm — a thin client over the REST API.
- Deploy: Docker Compose + a Caddy reverse proxy. Public instance: https://tela.cagdas.io

## How does an agent use tela?
Drop this in your `.mcp.json`:

```json
{
  "mcpServers": {
    "tela": {
      "command": "npx",
      "args": ["-y", "tela-mcp@latest"],
      "env": {
        "TELA_BASE_URL": "https://tela.cagdas.io",
        "TELA_API_KEY": "tela_pat_..."
      }
    }
  }
}
```

That's the whole integration. The agent then stops starting from zero every session — it reads the team's docs, writes back what it learns, and the next session (human or agent) picks up where it left off.

## How does tela compare?
- vs **Notion**: your docs are plain markdown on your own server, and agents get a real read/write API — not a closed block store with a chat sidebar. (Notion wins on databases, templates, polish.)
- vs **Confluence**: you run it yourself, no per-seat pricing, your data on your disk. (Confluence wins on Jira, granular permissions, enterprise scale.)
- vs **Obsidian**: built for a team — live multiplayer, a shared server, an agent API. (Obsidian wins on local-first single-user, plugins, graph.)
- vs **a git repo of markdown**: live WYSIWYG editing, instant full-text search, comments, sharing, and an agent API over the same markdown. (A repo wins on pure version control, free, zero infra.)
- vs **Outline**: a built-in MCP server makes agents first-class, on canonical markdown you host yourself. (Outline wins on maturity, Slack integration, track record.)

None of them combine all of it: an agent-native MCP server, markdown you own, live multiplayer, ranked full-text plus semantic search — that you run yourself.

## FAQ
- **Is the MCP integration real or a demo?** Real. `tela-mcp` is published on npm with 20 tools over the live REST API; the tool catalog and `.mcp.json` are in `mcp/README.md`, and the code is open.
- **Do I need Postgres?** Yes — tela runs on PostgreSQL, which `docker compose` brings up for you. Full-text search is built in (native Postgres `tsvector`); semantic search additionally uses an embedding model. No Elasticsearch, no external search service.
- **Can I get my content out?** Yes. Pages are canonical plain markdown — bulk-import a directory and export any time. No proprietary format.
- **How do agents authenticate?** Each agent uses a scoped personal access token (`tela_pat_…`) issued in Settings → API Keys — read, write, or admin, pinnable to a single space.
- **Is it multiplayer?** Yes — live collaborative editing over Yjs.

## Links
- Home: https://tela.cagdas.io/
- Live instance: https://tela.cagdas.io/login
- GitHub: https://github.com/zcag/tela
- MCP server (npm): https://www.npmjs.com/package/tela-mcp
- MCP docs: https://github.com/zcag/tela/blob/main/mcp/README.md
